$schema_note: Mock dataset for the tracing-UI take-home (~50 traces). A `trace` is one top-level execution of an LLM app. Each trace owns a tree of `spans` (runs) linked by `parentId` (null = root). Timestamps are ISO-8601 UTC over the window 2026-06-07 to 2026-06-14. `latencyMs` is a span's own wall-clock duration. IMPORTANT DETAILS: (1) `status` is `success` | `error` | `running`. A `running` trace/span is still in flight — it has NO `endTime` and NO `latencyMs` and a null `output`; design a live/in-progress state for these. (2) Sibling spans under the same parent may OVERLAP in time (e.g. parallel_research_agent fans out three retrievers at once, multi_agent_orchestrator runs workers concurrently) — your timeline/waterfall must handle overlap, not assume sequential. (3) Per-span token/cost fields (`model`, `promptTokens`, `completionTokens`, `totalTokens`, `costUsd`) appear only on `llm` spans. Each trace also carries its own `totalTokens`/`totalCostUsd` rollup — you can trust those or recompute them from the spans, just be consistent. (4) Many finished traces carry an optional `feedback` object (rating up/down, numeric score, optional comment). (5) The first ~10 traces are hand-crafted with rich inputs/outputs; the rest are generated for volume.
